title: Sampling from Stan models with PDMPSamplersR
text: start sampling 5-dimensional multivariate normal distribution. mean covariance drawn random passed Stan data flow parameters. Compare estimated true means covariances:
code: set.seed(123) d <- 5 mean_vec   <- rnorm(d, 0, 2) cov_matrix <- stats::rWishart(1, df = d + 2, Sigma = diag(d))[, , 1] stan_data  <- list(N = d, mu = mean_vec, sigma = cov_matrix)  data_path <- tempfile(fileext = ".json") write_stan_json(stan_data, data_path)  model_path <- path(stan_model_dir, "mvnormal.stan")  result <- pdmp_sample_from_stanmodel(   model_path, data_path,   flow = "ZigZag", T = 10000,   flow_mean = mean_vec, flow_cov = cov_matrix ) est_mean <- mean(result) est_cov  <- cov(result)  tib <- tibble(   truth    = c(mean_vec, cov_matrix[lower.tri(cov_matrix, diag = TRUE)]),   estimate = c(est_mean, est_cov[lower.tri(est_cov, diag = TRUE)]),   what     = rep(c("mu", "sigma"), times = c(d, d * (d + 1) / 2)) )  ggplot(tib, aes(x = truth, y = estimate)) +   geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "grey", alpha = .8) +   geom_point() +   facet_wrap(~what, scales = "free") +   labs(title = "ZigZag PDMP", x = "True value", y = "Estimated value")

title: Sampling from Stan models with PDMPSamplersR
text: target, using GridThinningStrategy algorithm.
code: result2 <- pdmp_sample_from_stanmodel(   model_path, data_path,   flow = "ZigZag", algorithm = "GridThinningStrategy", T = 10000 ) est_mean <- mean(result2) est_cov  <- cov(result2)  tib <- tibble(   truth    = c(mean_vec, cov_matrix[lower.tri(cov_matrix, diag = TRUE)]),   estimate = c(est_mean, est_cov[lower.tri(est_cov, diag = TRUE)]),   what     = rep(c("mu", "sigma"), times = c(d, d * (d + 1) / 2)) )  ggplot(tib, aes(x = truth, y = estimate)) +   geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "grey", alpha = .8) +   geom_point() +   facet_wrap(~what, scales = "free") +   labs(title = "ZigZag PDMP (Grid Thinning)", x = "True value", y = "Estimated value")

title: Sampling from Stan models with PDMPSamplersR
text: covariance matrix diagonal, variable selection sticky ZigZag exact. set different prior inclusion probabilities dimension check recovery.  covariance matrix diagonal, spike--slab approach produces biased results conditional distributions change variables excluded. known limitation.
code: set.seed(123) d <- 12 mean_vec   <- rep(0, d) cov_matrix <- stats::rWishart(1, df = d + 2, Sigma = diag(d))[, , 1] cov_matrix <- diag(diag(cov_matrix)) stan_data  <- list(N = d, mu = mean_vec, sigma = cov_matrix)  data_path <- tempfile(fileext = ".json") write_stan_json(stan_data, data_path)  prior_incl_prob <- seq(0.1, 0.9, length.out = d)  result3 <- pdmp_sample_from_stanmodel(   model_path, data_path,   flow = "ZigZag", T = 10000,   algorithm = "GridThinningStrategy",   sticky = TRUE, can_stick = rep(TRUE, d),   model_prior = bernoulli(prior_incl_prob),   parameter_prior = dnorm(rep(0, d), mean_vec, sqrt(diag(cov_matrix))) ) tib <- tibble(   true = prior_incl_prob,   est  = inclusion_probs(result3) )  ggplot(tib, aes(x = true, y = est)) +   geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "grey", alpha = .8) +   geom_point() +   labs(     title = "Variable selection (Bernoulli prior)",     x = "True inclusion probability",     y = "Estimated inclusion probability"   ) set.seed(123) d <- 12 mean_vec   <- rep(0, d) cov_matrix <- stats::rWishart(1, df = d + 2, Sigma = diag(d))[, , 1] stan_data  <- list(N = d, mu = mean_vec, sigma = cov_matrix)  data_path <- tempfile(fileext = ".json") write_stan_json(stan_data, data_path)  prior_incl_prob <- seq(0.1, 0.9, length.out = d)  result4 <- pdmp_sample_from_stanmodel(   model_path, data_path,   flow = "ZigZag", T = 10000,   algorithm = "GridThinningStrategy",   sticky = TRUE, can_stick = rep(TRUE, d),   model_prior = bernoulli(prior_incl_prob),   parameter_prior = dnorm(rep(0, d), mean_vec, sqrt(diag(cov_matrix))) ) tib <- tibble(   true = prior_incl_prob,   est  = inclusion_probs(result4) )  ggplot(tib, aes(x = true, y = est)) +   geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "grey", alpha = .8) +   geom_point() +   labs(     title = "Variable selection (non-diagonal — biased)",     x = "True inclusion probability",     y = "Estimated inclusion probability"   )

title: Sampling from Stan models with PDMPSamplersR
text: covariance matrix diagonal, spike--slab approach produces biased results conditional distributions change variables excluded. known limitation.
code: set.seed(123) d <- 12 mean_vec   <- rep(0, d) cov_matrix <- stats::rWishart(1, df = d + 2, Sigma = diag(d))[, , 1] stan_data  <- list(N = d, mu = mean_vec, sigma = cov_matrix)  data_path <- tempfile(fileext = ".json") write_stan_json(stan_data, data_path)  prior_incl_prob <- seq(0.1, 0.9, length.out = d)  result4 <- pdmp_sample_from_stanmodel(   model_path, data_path,   flow = "ZigZag", T = 10000,   algorithm = "GridThinningStrategy",   sticky = TRUE, can_stick = rep(TRUE, d),   model_prior = bernoulli(prior_incl_prob),   parameter_prior = dnorm(rep(0, d), mean_vec, sqrt(diag(cov_matrix))) ) tib <- tibble(   true = prior_incl_prob,   est  = inclusion_probs(result4) )  ggplot(tib, aes(x = true, y = est)) +   geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "grey", alpha = .8) +   geom_point() +   labs(     title = "Variable selection (non-diagonal — biased)",     x = "True inclusion probability",     y = "Estimated inclusion probability"   )

title: Sampling from Stan models with PDMPSamplersR
text: Instead independent Bernoulli priors inclusion, can use beta-binomial prior induces common inclusion probability across dimensions.
code: set.seed(123) d <- 7 mean_vec   <- rep(0, d) cov_matrix <- stats::rWishart(1, df = d + 2, Sigma = diag(d))[, , 1] cov_matrix <- diag(diag(cov_matrix)) stan_data  <- list(N = d, mu = mean_vec, sigma = cov_matrix)  data_path <- tempfile(fileext = ".json") write_stan_json(stan_data, data_path)  mp <- betabernoulli(1, 1) prior_incl_prob <- rep(mp$a / (mp$a + mp$b), d)  result5 <- pdmp_sample_from_stanmodel(   model_path, data_path,   flow = "ZigZag", T = 10000,   algorithm = "GridThinningStrategy",   sticky = TRUE, can_stick = rep(TRUE, d),   model_prior = mp,   parameter_prior = dnorm(rep(0, d), mean_vec, sqrt(diag(cov_matrix))) ) beta_binomial_pmf <- function(k, d, a, b, log = FALSE,                               individual_model = FALSE) {   log_binom  <- lfactorial(d) - lfactorial(k) - lfactorial(d - k)   log_beta_n <- lbeta(a + k, b + d - k)   log_beta_d <- lbeta(a, b)   log_prob   <- (!individual_model) * log_binom + log_beta_n - log_beta_d   if (log) log_prob else exp(log_prob) } samples <- discretize(result5)  tib_incl <- tibble(   true = prior_incl_prob,   est  = inclusion_probs(result5) ) plt_incl <- ggplot(tib_incl, aes(x = true, y = est)) +   geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "grey", alpha = .8) +   geom_point() +   labs(     title = "Inclusion probabilities",     x = "True inclusion probability",     y = "Estimated inclusion probability"   )  model_indicators <- apply(1L * (samples != 0), 1L, paste0, collapse = "") model_freq  <- table(model_indicators) model_probs <- model_freq / sum(model_freq) model_sizes <- nchar(gsub("0", "", names(model_freq), fixed = TRUE))  true_log_probs <- beta_binomial_pmf(   model_sizes, d, mp$a, mp$b,   log = TRUE, individual_model = TRUE ) est_log_probs <- log(unname(c(model_probs)))  tib_model <- tibble(   true_log = true_log_probs,   est_log  = est_log_probs ) plt_model <- ggplot(tib_model, aes(x = true_log, y = est_log)) +   geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "grey", alpha = .8) +   geom_point() +   labs(     title = "Model probabilities",     x = "True log model probability",     y = "Estimated log model probability"   )  plt_incl + plt_model

title: Sampling from Stan models with PDMPSamplersR
text: simulate binary data logistic model 10 predictors, 2 active. compare PDMP posterior frequentist GLM fit.
code: set.seed(123) n <- 200 d <- 10 d_on <- floor(0.2 * d) idx_on <- sample(1:d, d_on, FALSE) intercept <- -3 beta <- rep(0, d) beta[idx_on] <- rnorm(d_on, 0, 2) X <- scale(matrix(rnorm(n * d), n, d)) linpred <- intercept + X %*% beta y <- c(runif(n) <= plogis(linpred)) fit <- stats::glm(y ~ X, family = binomial())  model_path_lr <- path(stan_model_dir, "logistic_regression.stan")  sd_prior  <- 10 stan_data <- list(N = n, D = d, X = X, y = y, sd_prior = sd_prior) data_path <- tempfile(fileext = ".json") write_stan_json(stan_data, data_path)  result_lr <- pdmp_sample_from_stanmodel(   model_path_lr, data_path,   flow = "ZigZag", T = 20000,   algorithm = "GridThinningStrategy",   sticky = TRUE,   can_stick = c(FALSE, rep(TRUE, d)),   model_prior = bernoulli(0.5),   parameter_prior = dnorm(rep(0, d + 1), 0, sd_prior) ) samples <- discretize(result_lr)  tib_lr <- tibble(   true   = rep(c(intercept, beta), 2),   est    = c(mean(result_lr), coef(fit)),   how    = rep(c("Bayes", "Frequentist"), each = d + 1),   p_incl = c(inclusion_probs(result_lr), rep(1, d + 1)),   param  = rep(c("intercept", rep("beta", d)), 2) )  plt_recovery <- ggplot(tib_lr, aes(x = true, y = est, fill = how,                                    shape = param)) +   geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "grey", alpha = .8) +   geom_point(alpha = .5) +   scale_shape_manual(values = c(21, 24)) +   facet_wrap(~how) +   labs(title = "Parameter recovery", x = "True value", y = "Estimate")  plt_incl <- subset(tib_lr, how == "Bayes" & param == "beta") |>   ggplot(aes(x = est, y = p_incl)) +   geom_point() +   labs(     title = "Inclusion probability vs effect size",     x = "Posterior mean", y = "Inclusion probability"   )  plt_recovery / plt_incl

title: Sampling from Stan models with PDMPSamplersR
text: Finally, perform Bayesian two-sample t-test compare PDMP Bayes factor analytic result BayesFactor package.
code: set.seed(123) nx <- 30 ny <- 35 mux <- 0 muy <- 0.5 sigma2 <- 2.1  x1 <- rnorm(nx, mux, sqrt(sigma2)) x2 <- rnorm(ny, muy, sqrt(sigma2))  rscale <- 1.0 analytic_results <- BayesFactor::ttestBF(x = x1, y = x2, rscale = rscale)  model_path_tt <- path(stan_model_dir, "ttest.stan") stan_data <- list(nx = nx, ny = ny, x = x1, y = x2, rscale = rscale) data_path <- tempfile(fileext = ".json") write_stan_json(stan_data, data_path)  result_ttest <- pdmp_sample_from_stanmodel(   model_path_tt, data_path,   flow = "ZigZag", T = 100000,   algorithm = "GridThinningStrategy",   grid_n = 30,   sticky = TRUE,   can_stick = c(FALSE, TRUE, FALSE),   model_prior = bernoulli(0.5),   parameter_prior = rep(dcauchy(0, 0, rscale), 3) ) prior_inclusion_prob    <- 0.5 posterior_inclusion_prob <- inclusion_probs(result_ttest)[2] prior_odds    <- prior_inclusion_prob / (1 - prior_inclusion_prob) posterior_odds <- posterior_inclusion_prob / (1 - posterior_inclusion_prob) pdmp_bf <- posterior_odds / prior_odds  analytic_bf <- BayesFactor::extractBF(analytic_results, onlybf = TRUE)  tibble(   method = c("PDMP", "Analytic"),   bayes_factor = c(pdmp_bf, analytic_bf) ) # A tibble: 2 × 2   method   bayes_factor   <chr>           <dbl> 1 PDMP             1.36 2 Analytic         1.34

title: Sampling from Stan models with PDMPSamplersR
text: pdmp_result objects hold pointer PDMP trace lives Julia. pointer remains valid long Julia process lives, naively calling saveRDS() bare result later readRDS() leaves pointer dangling every subsequent estimator call fails. default, sampling functions (pdmp_sample, pdmp_sample_from_stanmodel, pdmp_sample_subsampled) immediately copy chain skeleton, .e., event times, positions, velocities, Julia R. costs little bit extra time memory mean saveRDS() / readRDS() work box want skip upfront copy, example running many short exploratory chains never save, memory constrained large model, pass materialize = FALSE: can still call materialize() manually later point extract skeleton enable saveRDS readRDS functionality.
code: result <- pdmp_sample(neg_grad, d = d, materialize = FALSE) # result$chains is a live Julia reference; calling saveRDS on this will not work

what: Fit a brms model using PDMP samplers — brm_pdmp
title: Fit a brms model using PDMP samplers — brm_pdmp
text: Uses PDMPSamplers.jl sampling backend brms models. Returns standard `brmsfit` object post-processing (`summary`, `plot`, `conditional_effects`, `loo`, etc.) working.

title: Fit a brms model using PDMP samplers — brm_pdmp
code: brm_pdmp(   formula,   data,   family = gaussian(),   prior = NULL,   flow = c("ZigZag", "BouncyParticle", "Boomerang", "AdaptiveBoomerang",     "PreconditionedZigZag", "PreconditionedBPS"),   algorithm = c("GridThinningStrategy", "ThinningStrategy", "RootsPoissonStrategy"),   adaptive_scheme = c("diagonal", "fullrank"),   T = 50000,   t0 = 0,   t_warmup = 0,   flow_mean = NULL,   flow_cov = NULL,   c0 = 0.01,   grid_n = 30,   grid_t_max = 2,   show_progress = TRUE,   discretize_dt = NULL,   n_chains = 1L,   threaded = FALSE,   compute_lp = FALSE,   subsample_size = NULL,   n_anchor_updates = 10L,   resample_dt = NULL,   hvp_mode = c("scaled", "none"),   use_hcv = FALSE,   use_anchor_bank = FALSE,   bank_capacity = 20L,   use_fd_hvp = FALSE,   post_warmup_simplify = FALSE,   use_fd_hcv = FALSE,   sticky = FALSE,   can_stick = NULL,   model_prior = NULL,   kappa = NULL,   stanvars = NULL,   sample_prior = "no",   save_model = NULL,   ... )

title: Fit a brms model using PDMP samplers — brm_pdmp
text: formula brms model formula. data data frame containing variables model. family family object (e.g., `gaussian()`, `bernoulli()`). prior `brmsprior` object NULL default priors. flow Character string specifying PDMP flow type. algorithm Character string specifying Poisson time strategy. adaptive_scheme Character string preconditioner adaptation. T Numeric total simulation time. t0 Numeric start time. t_warmup Numeric warmup duration. Auto-set adaptive flows.  subsampling active `t_warmup` 0, automatically set 20% sampling time. flow_mean Numeric vector flow reference mean, NULL. flow_cov Numeric matrix flow covariance, NULL. c0 Numeric thinning bound constant. grid_n Integer number grid points GridThinningStrategy. grid_t_max Numeric max grid interval GridThinningStrategy. show_progress Logical; show sampling progress bar. discretize_dt Numeric time step discretization, NULL automatic (yields ~1000 samples). n_chains Integer number chains (default: 1). multiple chains, `Rhat` multi-chain diagnostics become available. threaded Logical; run chains parallel (default: FALSE). compute_lp Logical; compute `lp__` via `BridgeStan::log_density()` sample (default: FALSE). Adds overhead enables `bridge_sampler()` populates `lp__` diagnostic column. subsample_size Integer number observations per subsample, NULL (default) full-data gradients. non-NULL, BridgeStan control-variate subsampled gradient used. Must less `nrow(data)`. Fixed-effects random-effects models supported subsampling. n_anchor_updates Integer number anchor updates warmup (default: 10). used `subsample_size` non-NULL. resample_dt Numeric time step resampling, NULL (default) resampling. used `subsample_size` non-NULL. hvp_mode Character string controlling Hessian-vector product scaling subsampled gradients. One `"scaled"` (default) `"none"`. use_hcv Logical; enable damped Hessian control variate (HCV) correction subsampled gradients (default: FALSE). Requires `subsample_size` set. Adds second-order correction reduces gradient variance near anchor. use_anchor_bank Logical; enable anchor bank LRU eviction subsampled gradients (default: FALSE). Requires `subsample_size` set. Maintains multiple cached anchor points selects nearest one trajectory step. bank_capacity Integer capacity anchor bank (default: 20). used `use_anchor_bank` TRUE. use_fd_hvp Logical; use finite-difference directional curvature instead BridgeStan's Hessian-vector product (default: FALSE). Replaces HVP call (2-3x gradient cost) one extra gradient call, giving ~30-50% per-grid-point savings. post_warmup_simplify Logical; switch fast constant-bound thinning mode warmup sampler well-adapted (default: FALSE). Activates reflection ratio 30% least 10 events observed. use_fd_hcv Logical; use finite-difference approximation HVP inside HCV correction (default: FALSE). Replaces exact BridgeStan HVP anchor cheaper gradient-based FD approximation. Also disables HVP grid thinning. used `use_hcv` TRUE. sticky Logical; enable spike--slab variable selection population-level coefficients (default: FALSE). Requires `model_prior` set. can_stick Optional logical vector indicating non-intercept population-level coefficients candidates selection. Length must match number supported coefficients. omitted, non-intercept population-level coefficients candidates. model_prior [bernoulli()] [betabernoulli()] object specifying prior model space. Required `sticky = TRUE`. kappa Optional numeric vector slab densities zero stickable coordinate (κ sticky PDMP literature). omitted, derived automatically brms prior specification (`normal(0, s)` `student_t(df, 0, s)` supported automatic derivation). stanvars Optional `stanvar` object custom Stan code. sample_prior Currently `""` supported. save_model Optional file path save generated Stan code. ... Additional arguments passed [brms::brm()] model setup.

title: Fit a brms model using PDMP samplers — brm_pdmp
text: `brmsfit` object.

title: Fit a brms model using PDMP samplers — brm_pdmp
text: PDMP samplers produce NUTS-style diagnostics. `lp__`, `accept_stat__`, related diagnostic columns set zero. Functions relying NUTS diagnostics (e.g., `pairs()` divergence plots, `nuts_params()`) produce meaningful output. `loo()` works brms computes `log_lik` directly model parameters, `lp__`. single chain, `Rhat` report `NA`. Use `n_chains >= 2` convergence diagnostics. `subsample_size` specified, function uses BridgeStan data-swapping compute control-variate subsampled gradients. centering fix applied brms-generated Stan code predictor centering remains consistent across subsamples.

what: Show the Stan code used by brm_pdmp — brm_stancode
title: Show the Stan code used by brm_pdmp — brm_stancode
text: Returns Stan code [brm_pdmp()] compile, subsampling modifications. Without `subsample_size`, identical `brms::stancode()`. `subsample_size`, two variants returned: standard standard model (used full-data     prior-models). ext_cpp model likelihood rewritten use     external C++ subsetting functions (used     subsampled-gradient model).

what: Inclusion Bayes factors for stickable coefficients — inclusion_bf
title: Inclusion Bayes factors for stickable coefficients — inclusion_bf
text: Computes inclusion Bayes factor stickable coefficient: $$BF_{\text{incl},k} = \frac{P(\gamma_k = 1 \mid y)}{P(\gamma_k = 0 \mid y)}       \cdot \frac{P(\gamma_k = 0)}{P(\gamma_k = 1)}$$

title: Inclusion Bayes factors for stickable coefficients — inclusion_bf
code: inclusion_bf(x, ...)  # Default S3 method inclusion_bf(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'brmsfit' inclusion_bf(x, ...)

text: x `brmsfit` fitted `sticky = TRUE` using version [brm_pdmp()] stores model prior sticky metadata. ... Passed [inclusion_prob()].

title: Inclusion Bayes factors for stickable coefficients — inclusion_bf
text: named numeric vector inclusion Bayes factors, one per   stickable coefficient.

title: Inclusion Bayes factors for stickable coefficients — inclusion_bf
text: answers "much data update prior odds favour inclusion?" Values greater 1 indicate evidence inclusion; values less 1 indicate evidence . Unlike inclusion probabilities alone, Bayes factor separates likelihood signal prior.

title: Model-averaged posterior means for stickable coefficients — model_averaged_mean

Use [inclusion_prob()] inspect corresponding inclusion probabilities.

title: PDMP Sampling — pdmp_sample
text: Performs Piecewise Deterministic Markov Process (PDMP) sampling using PDMPSamplers.jl Julia package.

title: PDMP Sampling — pdmp_sample
code: pdmp_sample(   f,   d,   flow = c("ZigZag", "BouncyParticle", "Boomerang", "AdaptiveBoomerang",     "PreconditionedZigZag", "PreconditionedBPS"),   algorithm = c("ThinningStrategy", "GridThinningStrategy", "RootsPoissonStrategy"),   T = 50000,   t0 = 0,   t_warmup = 0,   flow_mean = NULL,   flow_cov = NULL,   c0 = 0.01,   x0 = NULL,   theta0 = NULL,   hessian = NULL,   sticky = FALSE,   can_stick = NULL,   model_prior = NULL,   parameter_prior = NULL,   grid_n = 30,   grid_t_max = 2,   show_progress = TRUE,   n_chains = 1L,   threaded = FALSE,   adaptive_scheme = c("diagonal", "fullrank"),   materialize = TRUE )

title: PDMP Sampling — pdmp_sample
text: f Function computes NEGATIVE gradient. take numeric vector return numeric vector length. d Integer, dimension problem. flow Character string specifying flow type. One "ZigZag", "BouncyParticle", "Boomerang", "AdaptiveBoomerang", "PreconditionedZigZag", "PreconditionedBPS". `"AdaptiveBoomerang"` flow learns reference (mean precision) warmup requires `"GridThinningStrategy"` algorithm. `"PreconditionedZigZag"` `"PreconditionedBPS"` flows learn diagonal preconditioner warmup also require `"GridThinningStrategy"` algorithm. algorithm Character string specifying algorithm. One "ThinningStrategy", "GridThinningStrategy", "RootsPoissonStrategy". T Numeric, total sampling time (default: 50000). t0 Numeric, initial time (default: 0.0). t_warmup Numeric, warmup time (default: 0.0). Events warmup discarded. flow_mean Numeric vector length d, mean vector flow (default: zero vector). flow_cov Numeric matrix size d x d, covariance matrix flow (default: identity matrix). c0 Numeric, bound parameter (default: 1e-2). x0 Numeric vector length d, initial position (default: random normal). theta0 Numeric vector length d, initial velocity (default: random based flow). hessian Function returns negative Hessian matrix size d x d (default: NULL). used GridThinningStrategy compute Hessian-vector product. sticky Logical, whether use sticky sampling (default: FALSE). can_stick Logical vector length d, coordinates can stick (default: FALSE). model_prior Prior distribution object model selection. class 'bernoulli' 'beta-bernoulli' (default: NULL). parameter_prior Numeric vector length d, prior parameters sticky sampling (default: NULL). grid_n Integer, number grid points GridThinningStrategy (default: 30). grid_t_max Numeric, maximum time grid GridThinningStrategy (default: 2.0). show_progress Logical, whether show progress bar (default: TRUE). n_chains Integer, number chains run (default: 1). threaded Logical, whether run chains parallel (default: FALSE). adaptive_scheme Character string, adaptation scheme AdaptiveBoomerang. One "diagonal" (default, O(d) per update) "fullrank" (O(d^3) per update, better correlated targets). Ignored flow types. materialize Logical. TRUE (default), chain skeleton immediately extracted Julia R saveRDS() / readRDS() work without extra steps. Set FALSE skip extraction keep live Julia reference. can save time memory need save result plan call materialize() manually later.

title: PDMP Sampling — pdmp_sample
text: pdmp_result object. Use mean, var,   quantile, etc. continuous-time estimators,   discretize obtain sample matrix.

what: PDMP Sampling from Stan Model — pdmp_sample_from_stanmodel
title: PDMP Sampling from Stan Model — pdmp_sample_from_stanmodel
text: Performs Piecewise Deterministic Markov Process (PDMP) sampling Stan model using PDMPSamplers.jl Julia package BridgeStan integration.

title: PDMP Sampling from Stan Model — pdmp_sample_from_stanmodel
code: pdmp_sample_from_stanmodel(   path_to_stanmodel,   standata,   flow = c("ZigZag", "BouncyParticle", "Boomerang", "AdaptiveBoomerang",     "PreconditionedZigZag", "PreconditionedBPS"),   algorithm = c("ThinningStrategy", "GridThinningStrategy", "RootsPoissonStrategy"),   T = 50000,   t0 = 0,   t_warmup = 0,   flow_mean = NULL,   flow_cov = NULL,   c0 = 0.01,   x0 = NULL,   theta0 = NULL,   sticky = FALSE,   can_stick = NULL,   model_prior = NULL,   parameter_prior = NULL,   grid_n = 30,   grid_t_max = 2,   show_progress = TRUE,   n_chains = 1L,   threaded = FALSE,   adaptive_scheme = c("diagonal", "fullrank"),   materialize = TRUE )

title: PDMP Sampling from Stan Model — pdmp_sample_from_stanmodel
text: path_to_stanmodel Character, path Stan model file (.stan) compiled Stan model (./.dll/.dylib). .stan file provided, BridgeStan compile automatically. standata Either character path Stan data file (JSON format), named list written temporary JSON file via [write_stan_json()]. flow Character string specifying flow type. One "ZigZag", "BouncyParticle", "Boomerang", "AdaptiveBoomerang", "PreconditionedZigZag", "PreconditionedBPS". `"AdaptiveBoomerang"` flow learns reference (mean precision) warmup requires `"GridThinningStrategy"` algorithm. `"PreconditionedZigZag"` `"PreconditionedBPS"` flows learn diagonal preconditioner warmup also require `"GridThinningStrategy"` algorithm. algorithm Character string specifying algorithm. One "ThinningStrategy", "GridThinningStrategy", "RootsPoissonStrategy". T Numeric, total sampling time (default: 50000). t0 Numeric, initial time (default: 0.0). t_warmup Numeric, warmup time (default: 0.0). Events warmup discarded. flow_mean Numeric vector length d, mean vector flow (default: zero vector). flow_cov Numeric matrix size d x d, covariance matrix flow (default: identity matrix). c0 Numeric, bound parameter (default: 1e-2). x0 Numeric vector length d, initial position (default: random normal). theta0 Numeric vector length d, initial velocity (default: random based flow). sticky Logical, whether use sticky sampling (default: FALSE). can_stick Logical vector length d, coordinates can stick (default: FALSE). model_prior Prior distribution object model selection. class 'bernoulli' 'beta-bernoulli' (default: NULL). parameter_prior Numeric vector length d, prior parameters sticky sampling (default: NULL). grid_n Integer, number grid points GridThinningStrategy (default: 30). grid_t_max Numeric, maximum time grid GridThinningStrategy (default: 2.0). show_progress Logical, whether show progress bar (default: TRUE). n_chains Integer, number chains run (default: 1). threaded Logical, whether run chains parallel (default: FALSE). adaptive_scheme Character string, adaptation scheme AdaptiveBoomerang. One "diagonal" (default, O(d) per update) "fullrank" (O(d^3) per update, better correlated targets). Ignored flow types. materialize Logical. TRUE (default), chain skeleton immediately extracted Julia R saveRDS() / readRDS() work without extra steps. Set FALSE skip extraction keep live Julia reference. can save time memory need save result plan call materialize() manually later.

title: PDMP Sampling from Stan Model — pdmp_sample_from_stanmodel
text: pdmp_result object. Use mean, var,   quantile, etc. continuous-time estimators,   discretize obtain sample matrix.

title: PDMP Sampling from Stan Model — pdmp_sample_from_stanmodel
text: gradient Hessian-vector product automatically derived Stan model via BridgeStan extension PDMPSamplers.jl. means need provide separate gradient Hessian function.

what: PDMP sampling with subsampled gradients — pdmp_sample_subsampled
title: PDMP sampling with subsampled gradients — pdmp_sample_subsampled
text: Performs PDMP sampling using user-supplied subsampled gradient callback. low-level interface intended correctness verification prototyping. large-scale problems cheap likelihoods, consider Julia-native built-targets (available) avoid R--Julia call overhead.

title: PDMP sampling with subsampled gradients — pdmp_sample_subsampled
code: pdmp_sample_subsampled(   grad_sub,   n_obs,   d,   subsample_size,   flow = c("ZigZag", "BouncyParticle", "Boomerang", "AdaptiveBoomerang",     "PreconditionedZigZag", "PreconditionedBPS"),   algorithm = c("GridThinningStrategy", "ThinningStrategy", "RootsPoissonStrategy"),   T = 50000,   t0 = 0,   t_warmup = 0,   flow_mean = NULL,   flow_cov = NULL,   c0 = 0.01,   x0 = NULL,   hvp_sub = NULL,   grad_full = NULL,   use_full_gradient_for_reflections = FALSE,   grid_n = 30,   grid_t_max = 2,   show_progress = TRUE,   n_chains = 1L,   threaded = FALSE,   adaptive_scheme = c("diagonal", "fullrank"),   materialize = TRUE )

title: PDMP sampling with subsampled gradients — pdmp_sample_subsampled
text: grad_sub Function computes NEGATIVE subsampled gradient. take two arguments: numeric vector `x` (position) integer vector `indices` (1-based observation indices), return numeric vector length `d`. n_obs Integer, total number observations dataset. d Integer, dimension problem. subsample_size Integer, number observations per subsample. flow Character string specifying flow type. One "ZigZag", "BouncyParticle", "Boomerang", "AdaptiveBoomerang", "PreconditionedZigZag", "PreconditionedBPS". `"AdaptiveBoomerang"` flow learns reference (mean precision) warmup requires `"GridThinningStrategy"` algorithm. `"PreconditionedZigZag"` `"PreconditionedBPS"` flows learn diagonal preconditioner warmup also require `"GridThinningStrategy"` algorithm. algorithm Character string specifying algorithm. One "ThinningStrategy", "GridThinningStrategy", "RootsPoissonStrategy". T Numeric, total sampling time (default: 50000). t0 Numeric, initial time (default: 0.0). t_warmup Numeric, warmup time (default: 0.0). Events warmup discarded. flow_mean Numeric vector length d, mean vector flow (default: zero vector). flow_cov Numeric matrix size d x d, covariance matrix flow (default: identity matrix). c0 Numeric, bound parameter (default: 1e-2). x0 Numeric vector length d, initial position (default: random normal). hvp_sub Function subsampled Hessian-vector product (optional). Takes `(x, v, indices)` returns numeric vector length `d`. `NULL`, finite-difference approximation used. grad_full Function full (non-subsampled) NEGATIVE gradient (optional). Takes numeric vector `x` returns numeric vector length `d`. Required `use_full_gradient_for_reflections = TRUE`. use_full_gradient_for_reflections Logical, whether use full gradient reflection events (default: `FALSE`). grid_n Integer, number grid points GridThinningStrategy (default: 30). grid_t_max Numeric, maximum time grid GridThinningStrategy (default: 2.0). show_progress Logical, whether show progress bar (default: TRUE). n_chains Integer, number chains run (default: 1). threaded Logical, whether run chains parallel (default: FALSE). adaptive_scheme Character string, adaptation scheme AdaptiveBoomerang. One "diagonal" (default, O(d) per update) "fullrank" (O(d^3) per update, better correlated targets). Ignored flow types. materialize Logical. TRUE (default), chain skeleton immediately extracted Julia R saveRDS() / readRDS() work without extra steps. Set FALSE skip extraction keep live Julia reference. can save time memory need save result plan call materialize() manually later.

title: PDMP sampling with subsampled gradients — pdmp_sample_subsampled
text: pdmp_result object.

what: Write data to a JSON file readable by Stan — write_stan_json
title: Write data to a JSON file readable by Stan — write_stan_json
text: Write data JSON file readable Stan

title: Write data to a JSON file readable by Stan — write_stan_json
code: write_stan_json(data, file, always_decimal = FALSE)

title: Write data to a JSON file readable by Stan — write_stan_json
text: data (list) named list R objects. file (string) path data file written. always_decimal (logical) Force generate non-integers decimal points better distinguish integers floating point values. `TRUE` R objects `data` intended integers must integer type.

title: Write data to a JSON file readable by Stan — write_stan_json
text: Note: method copied cmdstanr package avoid dependency non-CRAN package. copied commit [edccf2d2f6449e7d80626a3ee6cc93845e82915b](https://github.com/stan-dev/cmdstanr/blob/edccf2d2f6449e7d80626a3ee6cc93845e82915b/R/data.R#L59). , file code therein follows license cmdstanr (BSD 3-Clause License), see LICENSE file cmdstanr details. `write_stan_json()` performs several conversions writing JSON file: * `logical` -> `integer` (`TRUE` -> `1`, `FALSE` -> `0`) * `data.frame` -> `matrix` (via [data.matrix()]) * `list` -> `array` * `table` -> `vector`, `matrix`, `array` (depending dimensions table) `list` `array` conversion intended make easier prepare data certain Stan declarations involving arrays: * `vector[J] v[K]` (equivalently `array[K] vector[J] v ` Stan 2.27) can constructed R list `K` elements element vector length `J` * `matrix[,J] v[K]` (equivalently `array[K] matrix[,J] m ` Stan 2.27 ) can constructed R list `K` elements element `IxJ` matrix can also passed R arrays instead lists list option provided convenience. Unfortunately arrays one dimension, e.g., `vector[J] v[K,L]` (equivalently `array[K,L] vector[J] v ` Stan 2.27) possible use R list array must used instead. example array R dimensions `KxLxJ`.

title: Write data to a JSON file readable by Stan — write_stan_json
code: x <- matrix(rnorm(10), 5, 2) y <- rpois(nrow(x), lambda = 10) z <- c(TRUE, FALSE) data <- list(N = nrow(x), K = ncol(x), x = x, y = y, z = z)  # write data to json file file <- tempfile(fileext = ".json") write_stan_json(data, file)  # check the contents of the file cat(readLines(file), sep = "\n") #> { #>   "N": 5, #>   "K": 2, #>   "x": [ #>     [-1.40004351672175, 1.14841160602606], #>     [0.25531705484526, -1.82181766097663], #>     [-2.43726361121953, -0.247325302073524], #>     [-0.00557128674616073, -0.244199606778383], #>     [0.621552721415214, -0.282705448814465] #>   ], #>   "y": [8, 11, 16, 4, 11], #>   "z": [1, 0] #> }   # demonstrating list to array conversion # suppose x is declared as `vector[3] x[2]` (or equivalently `array[2] vector[3] x`) # we can use a list of length 2 where each element is a vector of length 3 data <- list(x = list(1:3, 4:6)) file <- tempfile(fileext = ".json") write_stan_json(data, file) cat(readLines(file), sep = "\n") #> { #>   "x": [ #>     [1, 2, 3], #>     [4, 5, 6] #>   ] #> }
